common.tagline: Check label size and print scale

home.heroTitle: Print the right shipping label size before you buy postage.
home.heroDescription: Pick your marketplace, carrier, paper and printer. Get a clear 4×6, Letter or A4 recommendation with the exact scale, orientation and print-dialog settings to avoid cut-off, tiny or unscannable labels.
home.trustNote: Free checker first. No account, dashboard or label upload needed.
home.problemStatement: Solve shipping label size, print scale and printer-setting problems before you buy postage or reprint a carrier label.

pricing.metaDescription: Shipping Label Helper pricing for free label checks and local PDF tools.
pricing.h1: Start free. Upgrade only when the preview tools fit your workflow.
pricing.intro: The size checker, templates and troubleshooting guidance stay free. Paid features are the preview tools: PDF page-size checks, calibration sheets, barcode image review and test-print packs.
pricing.honestyText: Paid access uses Creem license keys. Keep your key safe because this site does not store user accounts.
pricing.checkoutReadyNote: Secure checkout is ready. After payment, enter the Creem license key from your receipt or customer portal.

free.name: Free
free.note: Good for sellers who need one quick label-size decision before printing.
free.feature1: Check marketplace, carrier, paper and printer
free.feature2: Calculate scale for tiny or oversized labels

pro.name: Pro Toolkit
pro.note: Unlock clean local outputs for sellers comparing PDF, barcode and calibration output before buying paid postage.
pro.feature1: Preview PDF page size and estimated scale
pro.feature2: Generate clean calibration sheets after unlock
pro.feature3: Download clean test print packs after unlock
pro.feature4: Review barcode quiet zones from an image

pricing.nextTitle: Need more than the free checker?
pricing.nextText: Start with the free label-size tools. Use advanced preview sections to decide whether PDF page-size checks, barcode image review or team workflow planning solve your actual print problem.
